category_title: Black eighth-grade students engaging in performances of resistance during writing revision
minimal_context: The student is reviewing the first draft of an essay with a tutor in a special education classroom to prepare a revised version for her general education class.
dialogue:
TUTOR: You have completed your first draft. Now we need to work on revising it so you have a piece of writing to take back to your general education class.

STUDENT: I already finished.

TUTOR: I have some suggestions for how you might revise it.

STUDENT: Why do I have to write it all again?

TUTOR: It is necessary to complete the task of drafting and revision.

STUDENT: (Rolls her eyes and raises her voice) I already finished! (Walks out of the classroom)

options.A: Draw on culturally sustaining pedagogies to ensure the student is writing about an essay topic that matters to her.
options.B: Require the student to participate in a sequenced writing project, writing four essays on the same subject over the semester.
options.C: Require that the essay undergoes three distinct revisions over time, ensuring that only the final, third draft is graded.
options.D: Have the student grade her own essay draft based on a four-point Likert scale from 'needs help' to 'personal best'.
